verdict: 2026-07-01 11:49:24 PDT — Not sound as-is. I would satisfy this story as a small declarative docs/bookkeeping change: user-facing 7.1.0+ warning, one internal decision record, archived matrix/guide banners, and dependency closure only if the manifest/lock actually prove the clean state. No product-code or new dependency shape issue, but the branch currently closes a dependency item on an invalid graph and leaves contradictory roadmap gates behind.

findings.title: Dependency closure is not backed by a clean graph
findings.claim: The esbuild/Vite item is marked DONE with prose saying the lockfile resolves vite 6.4.3 and a single esbuild 0.25.12, but the lock also contains vitest's nested vite 8.0.16. `npm ls vite esbuild --all` exits `ELSPROBLEMS` because that nested Vite wants esbuild `^0.27.0 || ^0.28.0` while the tree dedupes esbuild 0.25.12. That is not a clean dependency state to close a security/backlog item against.
findings.alternative: Either reconcile the manifest/lock so `npm ls vite esbuild --all` is clean, or leave/reword the backlog item to state the narrower verified fact and track the peer conflict separately. Use the dependency graph as the source of truth, not hand-written closure prose.
findings.win: Prevents a false DONE on dependency security work and gives future installs/audits one declarative, reproducible package state.

findings.title: Dropped gate still exists as roadmap guidance
findings.claim: The roadmap correctly declares the live-test matrix dropped elsewhere, but it still says rendering should be re-confirmed via the live-test matrix and still carries a Phase 6A camera-surface confirmation tied to that matrix. Meanwhile the matrix/guide say the surface-gate question is resolved. That leaves competing sources of truth about what is still a launch gate.
findings.alternative: Make `dev-docs/roadmap.md` the single planning authority: change the current-state row to accepted risk on clients below 7.1.0 with no matrix, and either remove the camera-surface gate if resolved or keep it as a separate Marketplace-config check with no matrix dependency. Leave matrix/guide content as archived reference only.
findings.win: Eliminates a zombie gate and prevents future work from resurrecting the dropped matrix or duplicating marketplace-surface research.
